name: Tony 's School Van
districts_or_customers: likely Lee County area, FL (Lehigh Acres)
recent_news: No public news found; very small operator with no confirmed website. Lee County / Lehigh Acres has multiple small van-based subcontractors (e.g. Lisdie Montano School Transportation) but Tony's School Van does not surface in public records.
fit_notes: Micro single-location van operator (~$1M revenue, 25 employees) in Lehigh Acres FL. Too small to be a meaningful tuck-in on its own; possibly relevant only as a route-acquisition/driver-pool deal if Summit expands in Lee County. Confidence low without confirmed ownership or fleet detail.

name: Another Magical School Bus
districts_or_customers: NYC DOE 3-K program (registered at 276 Grand Concourse, Bronx, DBN 12H260)
recent_news: Name appears on NYC MySchools as a 3-K/childcare program (12H260) rather than a yellow-bus contractor; related 'The Magical School Bus' at 2121 Chatterton Ave is a licensed group family day care (Nicole C. Antoine, OCFS).
fit_notes: Despite the 'school bus' branding, this entity appears to be an NYC childcare/3-K provider, not a pupil-transportation contractor. NOT a Summit tuck-in target — exclude from pipeline.

name: City Limousine
districts_or_customers: Okaloosa County, FL area (Mary Esther/Fort Walton Beach/Destin)
recent_news: No public news or website confirmed; not surfaced in Okaloosa County School District transportation materials. Name suggests livery/limousine rather than school-bus contractor; the 'special_needs' flag in source data may reflect NEMT/medical-livery work, not SPED yellow-bus routes.
fit_notes: Likely a small livery/limo and possibly NEMT operator in the Florida Panhandle, not a traditional school-bus contractor. Marginal fit for Summit unless it holds material SPED contracts with Okaloosa County — needs direct outreach to confirm; default would be exclude.

name: Promed
recent_news: No public news linking 2166 S Jog Rd Promed to school-district contracts. Multiple unrelated Florida 'ProMed' brands exist (ProMed Express / PROMEDXP, ProMed Management, Promed FL medical courier, ProMed Transport LLC), all positioned as non-emergency medical transportation, not pupil transport.
fit_notes: Appears to be a small NEMT/wheelchair-van operator in Palm Beach County, not a school-bus contractor. Not a Summit core tuck-in target; only relevant if it holds Palm Beach County SPED routes, which is unconfirmed.

name: Prime Medicar Svc
districts_or_customers: Chicago metro NEMT / Illinois HFS medicar program (assumed)
recent_news: No M&A or news found. 'Medicar' is an Illinois Medicaid-defined non-emergency medical transport category (HFS); company name strongly implies a wheelchair/sedan medicar provider rather than a CPS school-bus contractor. The Ravenswood suite address (#206) suggests a back-office only.
fit_notes: Micro (~10 employees, ~$0.75M revenue) Chicago medicar operator — NEMT, not yellow-bus pupil transport. Not a fit for Summit School Services tuck-in strategy; recommend exclude.
